name: Zucchini and Walnut Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Diced candied lemon peel, finely minced
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter the sides of an 8 x 2-inch cake pan and line the base with a circle of baking parchment. Butter the parchment.
Using a nut mill, grind walnuts to flour and set aside. Using the same nut mill, grind zucchini into fine shreds. There should be about 1 cup (6 ounces), firmly packed. Place in a sieve and press out the liquid.
Combine cornstarch, brown rice flour, baking powder, and salt; set aside.
Beat eggs with sugar until lemon-colored and very thick. A lifted beater should leave a slowly dissolving trail of batter.
Using a large rubber spatula, lightly fold in grated walnuts and minced lemon peel.
Fluff up zucchini shreds and fold in.
Sift cornstarch-flour mixture over batter and quickly fold in, retaining as much volume as possible.
Pour into prepared pan (the batter will be loose). Bake for 40 minutes until a tester comes out dry. Let cool in pan for 10 minutes before unmolding onto a rack. Peel off paper, reverse onto rack right side up, and let cool completely.
Dust with powdered sugar before serving.
Serves 8.
PER SERVING: 295 calories, 7g protein, 35g carbohydrate, 16 g fat (2 g saturated), 79 mg cholesterol, 103 mg sodium, 2 g fiber.
